title: A $7 trillion plan to share the AI era's wealth - TCR 06/19/26
description:
Senator Bernie Sanders unveiled a bill to tax the largest AI firms 50% and seed a $7 trillion sovereign wealth fund paying every American about $1,000 a year, US officials told ASML one of its banned EUV chip machines may have reached China while ASML says no such machine exists, and the population living with brain implants more than doubled to roughly 150 as Paradromics completed its first FDA-trial implant to restore speech.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-19-2026/

0:00 Brain implants surge and Paradromics
0:42 Sanders' $7 trillion AI public fund
6:01 Synthesis and Closing
9:12 Amazon engineers' civil-rights complaint
11:47 The Field That Crossed a Line
14:53 Midjourney's full-body ultrasound
17:43 Synthesis and Closing

This episode covers the Sanders $7 trillion AI public-ownership bill, the US-ASML EUV export-control dispute, three Amazon engineers filing a civil-rights complaint over data-center testimony, the brain-computer interface surge and Paradromics' Connexus implant, and Midjourney's pivot to a 60-second full-body ultrasound scanner.

A single day held one contest fought on five fronts: who controls the AI buildout, and who pays for it. Sanders' bill would tax the biggest AI firms by half to seed a $7 trillion fund and a yearly dividend, the largest public-ownership vehicle yet in a thread that runs through White House-OpenAI equity talks. The export-control fight with ASML tests how far a single-chokepoint regime can be enforced on an accusation officials will not show, even as Huawei charts a path around it. Three Amazon engineers who testified for data-center transparency say the company opened an internal investigation, and they have taken the case to a local civil-rights statute built to protect exactly that speech. Underneath the override fights, the thing being divided kept getting cheaper and wider: brain implants doubled toward 150 with the first speech-restoring FDA-trial device, Midjourney aimed idle compute at MRI-grade body scans, and a wind developer routed a $765 million buyout into buildable geothermal.
